jokes.text: Whoever is growing a small cannabis plot behind the gymnasium, congratulations. You just won a cruise. Please report to Security to collect your tickets.
jokes.setup: Standard school announcement format about someone growing marijuana on campus
jokes.punchline: Dean cheerfully announces they've 'won a cruise' (actually getting arrested)
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection with perfect bureaucratic deadpan delivery. The fake cheerfulness contrasted with obvious trap is well-crafted.

jokes.text: Campus news: the debate continues about the Library's PA system. Some students suggest lowering the volume, while others question its purpose entirely. We'll expand on this as it continues.
jokes.setup: News about library PA system debate
jokes.punchline: Reporting on PA system volume debate... over a PA system
jokes.explanation: Decent meta-joke about the medium being the message, but not particularly memorable or sharp.

jokes.text: I like it, makes every 10 minutes feel like the beginning of a TV series. The illusion only lasts until someone says something they'd never say on TV, like how much their life resembles TV. There goes everything.
jokes.setup: Commenting on how the Dean's announcements feel like TV
jokes.punchline: Breaking the fourth wall by acknowledging he's breaking the fourth wall
jokes.explanation: Perfect Abed character integration with sophisticated meta-commentary. Self-aware paradox delivered with characteristic detachment.

jokes.text: ¡Vamos! No uses esa palabra frente a Abed.
jokes.setup: Someone uses the word 'retardo' (lateness/delay)
jokes.punchline: Shirley thinks it's offensive to Abed (implies he's developmentally disabled)
jokes.explanation: Character-driven misunderstanding. Shirley's protective instinct creates the humor through her assumption.

jokes.text: Come on! Don't use that word in front of Abed.
jokes.setup: Someone says Jeff is 'retarded' (late)
jokes.punchline: Treating 'retarded' as offensive specifically because of Abed's meta-awareness
jokes.explanation: Character-specific humor playing on Abed's unique perspective, though the setup is a bit forced.

jokes.text: I hope your mom didn't make you bathe together because one of you would be 30.
jokes.setup: Pierce claiming he and Jeff have a 'brotherly connection'
jokes.punchline: Pointing out the age gap makes 'brotherly bathing' creepy
jokes.explanation: Good economical punchline that deflates Pierce's delusion about his connection to Jeff.

jokes.text: Probably Jeff is late to avoid having to deal with your retardation.
jokes.setup: Continued discussion about Jeff being late
jokes.punchline: Brutal honesty about Pierce being the reason Jeff avoids them
jokes.explanation: Functional cringe moment but relies on crude language rather than clever writing.

jokes.text: Maybe you skipped a column. What's happening in Guatemala? - Nothing. - Are they killing journalists?
jokes.setup: Britta mentions journalists being killed in Guatemala
jokes.punchline: Others confused about the random reference, Britta deflects awkwardly
jokes.explanation: Sets up Britta's performative activism but the delivery is somewhat clunky.

jokes.text: Real stories... don't have spoilers. Do you understand that TV and real life are different, right?
jokes.setup: Abed calls real news about Guatemala a 'spoiler'
jokes.punchline: Having to explain to Abed that reality doesn't work like TV
jokes.explanation: Perfect Abed character moment - his TV-brain applied to real world creates absurd but logical confusion.

jokes.text: My Lady. - My Lord. - Hello baby. - Hello sweetness. - You smell good. Vitamin P. - Good day, Jeffrey.
jokes.setup: Jeff makes a dramatic entrance
jokes.punchline: Everyone ironically plays along with increasingly silly greetings
jokes.explanation: Good group dynamic comedy showing how they mock Jeff's pretensions through over-the-top responses.

jokes.text: I never have anything to share. I swear my life is emptier than this filing cabinet.
jokes.setup: Group talking about sharing their problems
jokes.punchline: Jeff comparing his empty life to a literal empty filing cabinet
jokes.explanation: Good self-deprecating humor that reveals Jeff's awareness of his shallow existence.

jokes.text: What does someone have to do to get a 'C' around here?
jokes.setup: Jeff asking for notes to fill his empty folder
jokes.punchline: Jeff's low academic standards revealed - he just wants a C
jokes.explanation: Character-consistent joke about Jeff's minimal effort approach to education.

jokes.text: if you interpret 'grandchildren' as a metaphor for 'friendship', you'll agree with that Transformer over there that it's time for me to become a man. - Reading the Torah.
jokes.setup: Pierce explaining greeting cards don't exist for his situation
jokes.punchline: Bizarre metaphor involving Transformers and Torah reading
jokes.explanation: Peak Pierce rambling logic - connecting random elements in a way that somehow makes sense to him.

jokes.text: She said I was cute.
jokes.setup: Britta calls Jeff cute but also selfish and narcissistic
jokes.punchline: Jeff only hears the 'cute' part, ignoring all criticism
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jeff character moment - his selective hearing and ego perfectly captured in minimal words.

jokes.text: I'm not really a sausage-fest kind of guy. Maybe some other time. - Raincheck.
jokes.setup: Pierce inviting Jeff to a 'sausage fest' (guys' night)
jokes.punchline: Jeff's uncomfortable rejection of Pierce's desperate attempt at bonding
jokes.explanation: Solid cringe moment showing the awkwardness between Jeff and Pierce, though not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Tú eres como Jodie Foster o Susan Sarandon. / Mejor que sea real a que sea lindo.
jokes.setup: Annie compares Britta to activist actresses
jokes.punchline: Britta's backhanded acceptance focusing on authenticity over attractiveness
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Britta's insecurity about attractiveness vs. substance, typical of her character.

jokes.text: like building a wall that can be seen from space.
jokes.setup: Chang sarcastically listing what students expect him to teach
jokes.punchline: Reference to Great Wall of China stereotype
jokes.explanation: Decent racial stereotype commentary, though the Great Wall reference is somewhat obvious.

jokes.text: In Spanish, my nickname is... [long pause] ...because my wisdom could devour you!
jokes.setup: Chang building up to reveal his Spanish nickname
jokes.punchline: Never actually says the nickname, just explains why he has it
jokes.explanation: Excellent awkward pause comedy - Chang's grandiose buildup to nothing reveals his insecurity perfectly.

jokes.text: So don't question Mr. Chang or he'll bite you. Yes, I'll bite you. Yes, I bite!
jokes.setup: Chang threatening students not to question him
jokes.punchline: Escalating threat of biting gets more emphatic and unhinged
jokes.explanation: Good escalating absurdity showing Chang's unhinged authority complex through increasingly bizarre threats.

jokes.text: Do you understand, 'Star Burns'?
jokes.setup: Chang addressing a student
jokes.punchline: Calling him 'Star Burns' instead of his name based on his sideburn design
jokes.explanation: Good visual character gag - Chang's casual cruelty in nicknaming students after their appearance.

jokes.text: I've been eyeing it since day one when we registered. - Okay. Give me your card. - I want to put it on now.
jokes.setup: Abed explaining why he wants Jeff's shirt
jokes.punchline: Abed's immediate demand to wear it right now
jokes.explanation: Good Abed character beat - his directness and lack of social inhibition about immediate gratification.

jokes.text: You know what? I did trade cards. Can you believe it? What are the odds?
jokes.setup: Jeff denying he rigged the partner selection
jokes.punchline: Immediately admits he did trade cards, repeating 'what are the odds'
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing Jeff's inability to maintain lies and his smug satisfaction.

jokes.text: Can you believe it? / Not really. But you do, so that's fine.
jokes.setup: Pierce asks if Shirley believes his sperm story
jokes.punchline: Shirley's polite but skeptical response
jokes.explanation: Perfect Shirley response - polite but clearly doesn't believe him. Great economy and character voice.

jokes.text: Do you think I'm garbage?
jokes.setup: Jeff trying to have a normal conversation about Spanish homework
jokes.punchline: Pierce's sudden, desperate question about his worth
jokes.explanation: Excellent cringe moment capturing Pierce's desperate need for validation in the most awkward way possible.

jokes.text: Just look at her name. What is she, a water filter?
jokes.setup: Pierce trying to convince Jeff to forget about Britta
jokes.punchline: Comparing 'Britta' to the water filter brand
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay joke that works on multiple levels and establishes a series-long running gag.

jokes.text: Even better, before we do that, why don't we ask ourselves... what is a story? / Oh my God in Heaven.
jokes.setup: Pierce wants to overthink their simple Spanish assignment
jokes.punchline: Jeff's exasperated religious exclamation
jokes.explanation: Perfect reaction to Pierce's pretentious overthinking of a simple task.

jokes.text: I don't do anything. What can I do? You can hang the Chacata-Panecos piñata. You're aware they beat him to death, right? That's where we got the idea. It's touching.
jokes.setup: Making a piñata of the murdered journalist
jokes.punchline: Calling it 'touching' to beat a piñata representing someone who was beaten to death
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy showing the group's complete tonal deafness to the serious subject matter.

jokes.text: We have something incredibly long, and very confusing and a little bit homophobic. And really, really, specifically, surprisingly and gratuitously critical of Israel. It's called 'two conquistadores', probably should be 'dos'. I mean, it's a Spanish class.
jokes.setup: Jeff describing their Spanish assignment
jokes.punchline: Escalating list of problems culminating in basic Spanish error
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalating comedy building to perfect punchline about basic Spanish failure in Spanish class.

jokes.text: Why does everyone have tape on their mouth? It's a protest, Pierce. Good. Because I know what I'd like to protest... How much you offended me.
jokes.setup: Pierce arriving at the silent protest
jokes.punchline: Pierce wants to protest being offended by Jeff, missing the point entirely
jokes.explanation: Good Pierce character comedy - his self-centeredness turning a political protest into personal grievance.

jokes.text: I'll show you some sperm, buddy.
jokes.setup: Pierce getting increasingly agitated at Jeff
jokes.punchline: Pierce's bizarre threat involving his 'hyper-virile' sperm
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Pierce's earlier sperm story, showing how his delusions manifest in confrontation.

jokes.text: Pierce, what we've created... is bigger than both of us, and it deserves to be done well.
jokes.setup: Jeff trying to convince Pierce to perform their terrible Spanish assignment
jokes.punchline: Jeff treating their awful project as if it's important art
jokes.explanation: Good Jeff character moment showing his ability to apply his lawyer rhetoric to absurd situations.

jokes.text: Why are there costumes? These are short dialogues, you're not supposed to... - Leave you breathless? Well, it will.
jokes.setup: Chang confused about elaborate costumes for simple assignment
jokes.punchline: Pierce's overconfident response about their performance quality
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the disaster to come, showing Pierce's delusional confidence.

jokes.text: 'F'. 'F' para abajo. ¿Qué? ¿Acaso dijo 'S'?
jokes.setup: Chang giving them an F grade
jokes.punchline: Creates a grade worse than F, then questions if he said something else
jokes.explanation: Great Chang moment - creating impossible grades then confusing himself. The 'F minus' concept is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: You have that thing you do with your face when you're trying to be funny that makes people think about how cool you think you are.
jokes.setup: Pierce giving Jeff feedback on their performance
jokes.punchline: Pierce's surprisingly accurate critique of Jeff's performative humor
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment - Pierce showing unexpected insight into Jeff's persona while still being Pierce.
